title: Button Component
content:
The `Button` component provides a versatile and stylable button element for the UI. It leverages `class-variance-authority` to manage different visual variants and sizes. ## Purpose To create a consistent and customizable button across the application, allowing for various styles (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link) and sizes (default, small, large, icon). ## Key Exports * **`Button`**: The main React component for rendering buttons. * **`buttonVariants`**: A utility function from `class-variance-authority` that generates CSS classes based on provided variants and sizes. ## Props ### `ButtonProps` This interface extends standard `React.ButtonHTMLAttributes` and includes properties for controlling the button's appearance: * **`variant`** (`'default' | 'destructive' | 'outline' | 'secondary' | 'ghost' | 'link'`): Defines the visual style of the button. * **`size`** (`'default' | 'sm' | 'lg' | 'icon'`): Determines the size of the button. * **`asChild`** (`boolean`): If `true`, renders the button using the `Slot` component from Radix UI, allowing for composition. * **`className`** (`string`): Allows for custom CSS classes to be applied. ## Usage ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; function ExampleButtons() { return (
); } ``` ## `buttonVariants` Utility The `buttonVariants` function can be used independently to generate class names for more granular control or when not using the `Button` component directly. ```ts import { buttonVariants } from '@/components/ui/button'; const myButtonClasses = buttonVariants({ variant: 'outline', size: 'lg' }); // myButtonClasses will be a string of CSS classes ```
